The Bank Job (2008)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $30,060,660Details
International Box Office $36,082,345
Worldwide Box Office $66,143,005
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,875,479
Total $17,875,479
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Self-reformed petty criminal Terry Leather has become a financially struggling car dealer and settled into a pedestrian London life with his wife and kids, but takes the plunge into big crime when his ex-girlfriend, Martine, turns up with an offer to pull off a lucrative bank heist. After Terry assembles his crew of misfits and begins the operation, he finds that there are other agendas at play, and powerful players who have designs on the vault’s contents.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,935,256 (19.7% of total gross)
Legs:5.06 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,603 opening theaters/1,613 max. theaters, 6.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $50,073,183

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 7th, 2008 (Wide) by Lionsgate
Video Release: July 15th, 2008 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, nudity, violence and language
Running Time: 110 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Epilogue
Plot point: Bank Robbery, Blackmail, Corrupt Cops, Heist, Kidnap, Tortured for Information
Social setting: Crime Caper, Organized Crime
Subgenre: Crime Thriller
Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Relativity Media
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

Self-reformed petty criminal Terry Leather has become a financially struggling car dealer and settled into a pedestrian London life with his wife and kids, but takes the plunge into big crime when his ex-girlfriend, Martine, turns up with an offer to pull off a lucrative bank heist. After Terry assembles his crew of misfits and begins the operation, he finds that there are other agendas at play, and powerful players who have designs on the vault’s contents.
